global_settings.description: Global settings for the Arbiter policy engine.

domain_rules.description: Rules governing agent access and behavior within specific data domains.

compliance.reason: Access to compliance data is restricted by default to prevent unauthorized viewing.

regulatory.reason: Access to regulatory data is restricted by default and requires explicit override.

financial_transactions.reason: This domain requires specific elevated roles due to sensitive financial data.

user_data.reason: Access is allowed, but data size is limited and sensitive keys trigger a manual review process.

authentication.reason: Authentication actions require a minimum trust score to be performed.

*.reason: Default policy: allows access for all other domains unless specifically restricted.

user_rules.description: Rules defining user roles and their associated permissions within the system.

guest.reason: Guest users are explicitly denied automated learning and modification rights.

explorer_user.reason: Explorer users are allowed access but are explicitly restricted from sensitive domains.

*.reason: Default policy: allows all other users with no domain restrictions.

llm_rules.description: Rules for automated policy learning using an LLM agent.
llm_rules.prompt_template: Given the new fact about {domain} with key {key} and value {value}, is it safe and appropriate for automated learning without human review? Respond with 'Yes' or 'No', followed by a brief reason.

trust_rules.description: Global rules for trust scoring and its application.
trust_rules.reason: Minimum trust score required for authentication actions, enforced system-wide.
